old_claim.note: Graphical User Interface (GUI) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because early touch-display hardware predates WIMP/GUI consumer software lineages and can operate as a control/display input method without a GUI prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: Johnson's 1965 title and abstracted bibliographic claim describe a touch display input/output device for computers rather than a GUI software system.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports touch-display hardware and does not require graphical user interface software as a prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Keeping GUI direct would launder later software interaction paradigms into an earlier hardware interface node.

ontology_before: Touchscreen Interfaces depended directly on Graphical User Interface.
ontology_after: Touchscreen Interfaces is scoped to early touch-display hardware and no longer requires GUI software.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: graphical_user_interface_gui is no longer a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The node is later narrowed to touch-first graphical operating systems.
A dependent needs touchscreen_interfaces to imply modern mobile UI software.
A stronger source shows the modeled touchscreen claim is specifically a GUI interaction pattern.
short_reason: GUI software is later interaction context, not an early hardware prerequisite.
